concerns.text: Task T024 is duplicated. It appears in Phase 4 (Implementation for US2) as 'Implement "Choice-Only" specific documentation...' and again in Phase 5 (Tests for US3) as 'Contract test for comparison report schema...'. The ID T024 is used twice with different descriptions, creating ambiguity in execution and traceability.

Task T029, T030, and T031 reference specific 'Reviewer Responses' (Aristotle, Kahneman, Socrates) as implementation steps. While the Spec (FR-006, FR-008) requires framing and proxy controls, these tasks lack explicit `[FR-NNN]` or `[SC-NNN]` tags linking them to the specific requirements they satisfy. The description implies the link, but the coverage protocol requires explicit tagging or unambiguous description matching to ensure the FR/SC is not missed if the prose changes.

Task T015 (VIF calculation) addresses the Edge Case regarding collinearity, but lacks an explicit `[FR-NNN]` or `[SC-NNN]` tag. While the Edge Case is covered, the Functional Requirement FR-008 (proxy controls) and Success Criterion SC-004 (VIF diagnostics) are not explicitly tagged on this task, risking a coverage gap if the Edge Case text is modified.

Task T022 (5-fold cross-validation) addresses SC-002, but lacks an explicit `[SC-002]` tag. The description mentions 'ensure ≥95% convergence rate', which matches SC-002, but the lack of a tag makes automated coverage verification difficult.

Task T028 (Bonferroni correction) addresses FR-007, but lacks an explicit `[FR-007]` tag. The description mentions 'multiple-comparison correction', which matches FR-007, but the lack of a tag makes automated coverage verification difficult.
